channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Electronegativity and entropy design of layered oxides for sodium-ion batteries
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 08 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68016-5

The structure of layered oxides dictates their properties as positive electrodes in sodium-ion batteries, but its rational design remains challenging. Here, authors employ electronegativity and configurational entropy as a design descriptor to guide the synthesis of a sodium-deficient layered oxide with an O3 stacking sequence, rendering good thermal and air stability.
item.dc:title: Electronegativity and entropy design of layered oxides for sodium-ion batteries
item.dc:creator: Lu Gan
Hu-Rong Yao
Jingwen Dai
Zefeng Lai
Xin-Guang Yuan

Chuying OuYang
Yu-Guo Guo

item.title: Boosted catalytic activity by metal-oxide interface in porous single-crystalline niobium oxide monoliths
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 07 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68117-1

Introducing and stabilizing oxygen vacancies within oxide supports remains a significant challenge for enhancing catalytic activity in redox reactions. Here, the authors fabricate centimeter-scale porous single-crystalline Nb₂O₅ monoliths and deposit Pt clusters on their surfaces, creating an interfacial system that markedly improves catalytic CO oxidation.
item.dc:title: Boosted catalytic activity by metal-oxide interface in porous single-crystalline niobium oxide monoliths
item.dc:creator: Xiangqi Gao
Guoliang Ma
Cong Luo
Lingting Ye
Kui Xie
